content.text:
# SSH Key Pair Generation

Generate an SSH key pair in the files `~/.ssh/id_rsa` and `~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub`.

Don't make them password protected.

content.thinking: The user wants me to generate an SSH key pair (RSA) at ~/.ssh/id_rsa (private key) and ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub (public key), without a passphrase.

content.text:
Generating public/private rsa key pair.
Your identification has been saved in /root/.ssh/id_rsa
Your public key has been saved in /root/.ssh/id_rsa.pub
The key fingerprint is:
SHA256:Jtgjvd7XzhORFI9SVutKhcAL7cTGjPdiU5dOQ04vV0Y root@96CTBC4
The key's randomart image is:
+---[RSA 4096]----+
|         B. +++oE|
|        o X+o*=+.|
|         *.=o=*.o|
|     +    *.+o.o |
|    o = S. o...  |
|     . =   ...   |
|      .    ...   |
|     . .  ..o    |
|      . .. .o.   |
+----[SHA256]-----+

content.thinking: The SSH key pair has been generated successfully. Let me verify the files exist.

content.text:
Done! The SSH key pair has been generated:

- **Private key:** `~/.ssh/id_rsa` (RSA 4096-bit, no passphrase)
- **Public key:** `~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub`
